title: Chettinad Mutton Chukka

subs.name: 600 g chicken with bone — reduce to 3 whistles
reduce to 1 tsp for medium heat

steps.text: Dry roast the black peppercorns and fennel seeds in the kadai for 2 minutes on low heat until fragrant. Transfer to a mixie, add 2 tablespoons of water and grind to a coarse paste. Set aside.

In a pressure cooker, heat 2 tablespoons of gingelly oil. Add the whole spices — star anise, cinnamon, cloves — and stir for 30 seconds. Add half the shallots and fry until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook 1 minute.

Add the mutton pieces, turmeric, a pinch of red chilli powder and salt. Stir to coat, then add 200 ml water. Seal the pressure cooker and cook on high heat until 5 whistles, then reduce to low for 10 minutes. Allow pressure to release naturally.

While the mutton cooks, heat the remaining 2 tablespoons of gingelly oil in the kadai. Add the remaining shallots and fry until deeply golden, about 6 minutes. Add the tomatoes and cook until they break down and oil separates.
Add the remaining red chilli powder and coriander powder. Stir for 30 seconds, then add the pressure-cooked mutton along with any residual stock. Stir everything together.

Add the curry leaves and stir in the pepper-fennel paste. Raise the flame to high and stir-fry, tossing the mutton, for 8 minutes until the liquid evaporates completely and the masala clings dry to each piece.
Taste and adjust salt. The mutton should be nearly dry, each piece coated in dark masala. Scatter coriander leaves and serve with rice, parotta or as a standalone appetiser.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
Follow the cooker manufacturer's fill, venting and opening instructions; never force the lid open under pressure.
critical_checks: Use an appropriate food thermometer and safe doneness guidance for the specific cut of meat.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: mutton → 600 g chicken with bone — reduce to 3 whistles; gingelly-oil → coconut oil; black-pepper → reduce to 1 tsp for medium heat; shallot → 2 medium onions, finely chopped
